name: widget-bundle-size
question: Is the built feedbackmonk widget bundle (widget/dist/*.{js,mjs,css}) at most 30720 bytes, and does it contain zero canonical third-party tracker hostnames? Has the canonical tracker-list drifted from its hashed baseline?

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <n> with size detail + tracker-hit file:line offenders on FAIL; tracker-list SHA-256 always printed; exit code 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on environment failure

consumer_scope: P2+ widget/ (every commit touching widget/dist/ or widget/src/; CI gate from P2 start)
fallback: Probe A: ls -la widget/dist/*.{js,mjs,css} and sum byte counts; compare to 30720. Probe B: grep -i -F -f expected-trackers.txt widget/dist/* . Drift hash: sort + lowercase + join-with-\n the non-comment non-blank lines of expected-trackers.txt and SHA-256 the UTF-8 bytes.

provenance.authored_by: CLAUDE-A worker (P2 Customer-Facing, Task Zero — collab-20260514-035703)
provenance.rationale: FR-FBR-04 (<30KB bundle) + DEC-FBR-02 (no third-party trackers brand promise). Three-leg defense per D-FBR-02 pattern: (1) vite.config.ts terser+CSP-safe bundler config; (2) THIS oracle (size probe + tracker-hostname probe + list-hash drift detection); (3) Playwright + axe-core a11y harness for behavioural regressions. The cap is reified as a code-level invariant per arc-plan Testability Gate Q3=4 finding: without the oracle the agent would accept 'looks small enough' and ship over-budget.
